nodes.title: Backpack Buddies Weekend Food Program

body.processed:
Backpack Buddies provides food-insecure children with backpacks filled with nutritious, easy-to-prepare meals and snacks for weekends and school breaks when school meals are unavailable.

Program Details

  • Discreetly distributed through partner schools every Friday
  • Each backpack contains 6 meals and snacks for the weekend
  • Foods selected for child-friendliness and nutritional value
  • No cooking required - all items can be eaten cold or at room temperature

Impact

Currently serving 2,400 children at 45 schools across Riverside County. Teachers report improved attendance, behavior, and academic performance among participating students.
body.summary: Backpack Buddies provides food-insecure children with backpacks filled with nutritious, easy-to-prepare meals and snacks for weekends and school breaks when school meals are unavailable.Program Detail

eligibility.processed: Children enrolled at participating Riverside County schools who are identified by school staff as experiencing food insecurity. Participation is confidential.
eligibility.summary: Children enrolled at participating Riverside County schools who are identified by school staff as experiencing food insecurity. Participation is confidential.

nodes.schedule: Weekly during the school year (September - June)

body.processed:
The Golden Harvest program ensures seniors in our community have access to nutritious food through monthly food boxes, home delivery, and congregate meal programs.

Services

  • Monthly food box with shelf-stable items tailored to senior dietary needs
  • Fresh produce delivery for homebound seniors
  • Weekly congregate meals at 8 senior centers
  • Nutrition counseling and health screenings
  • Benefits enrollment assistance (SNAP, Medicaid)

Impact

The Golden Harvest program serves over 3,800 seniors each month, many of whom are living on fixed incomes and must choose between buying food and paying for medications or utilities.
body.summary: The Golden Harvest program ensures seniors in our community have access to nutritious food through monthly food boxes, home delivery, and congregate meal programs.ServicesMonthly food box with shelf-s

eligibility.processed: Riverside County residents age 60 and older. Priority given to homebound seniors and those with limited income. No documentation required to receive food assistance.
eligibility.summary: Riverside County residents age 60 and older. Priority given to homebound seniors and those with limited income. No documentation required to receive food assistance.

nodes.title: Community Kitchen Job Training

body.processed:
The Community Kitchen program provides culinary job training to adults facing barriers to employment while preparing nutritious meals for our meal distribution programs.

Training Components

  • 12-week culinary skills training program
  • Food safety certification (ServSafe)
  • Kitchen management and inventory skills
  • Resume writing and interview preparation
  • Job placement assistance with restaurant and catering partners

Dual Impact

Participants gain valuable job skills while producing over 500 meals per day for our community meal programs. Last year, 85% of graduates were placed in food service employment within 90 days of completing the program.
body.summary: The Community Kitchen program provides culinary job training to adults facing barriers to employment while preparing nutritious meals for our meal distribution programs.Training Components12-week culi

eligibility.processed: Adults 18 and older facing barriers to employment including but not limited to history of homelessness, incarceration, or substance recovery. Must commit to attending 5 days per week for 12 weeks.
eligibility.summary: Adults 18 and older facing barriers to employment including but not limited to history of homelessness, incarceration, or substance recovery. Must commit to attending 5 days per week for 12 weeks.

nodes.schedule: New cohorts begin quarterly (January, April, July, October)
